name: upReach Associate Programme
description: Two-year programme supporting undergraduates from underrepresented backgrounds to access top graduate careers through skills coaching, employer connections, and mentoring.
eligibility: UK undergraduates from low socioeconomic backgrounds, first-generation students, or those from underrepresented ethnic groups.

name: Sutton Trust Summer Schools
description: Free residential summer schools at top UK universities for high-achieving students from low-income families who may not have considered higher education.
eligibility: Year 12 students (England/Wales) or S5 (Scotland) from families earning under £25,000.

name: SEO London Career Accelerator
description: Connects Black, Asian, and minority ethnic students and graduates with careers in finance, law, and technology through skills bootcamps and employer placements.
eligibility: Black, Asian, and minority ethnic students in their penultimate year of university.

name: 10,000 Black Interns
description: Transforms the horizons and ambitions of young Black people in the UK by offering paid internships across a wide range of industries.
eligibility: Black students or recent graduates (within 2 years) with the right to work in the UK.

name: Rare Recruitment Contextual Recruitment System
description: Helps employers identify high-potential candidates who have achieved highly despite socioeconomic disadvantage. Used by 100+ top graduate employers.
eligibility: Students who attended a non-selective state school, received free school meals, or are first-generation university students.

name: City Hive Diversity in Investment
description: Dedicated to increasing diversity in the investment management industry. Runs mentoring, work experience, and networking events for underrepresented groups.
eligibility: Students from BAME backgrounds, women, and those from low socioeconomic backgrounds interested in investment.

name: Aspiring Solicitors
description: Opens doors for talented individuals from underrepresented backgrounds into the legal profession through virtual and in-person internships at top law firms.
eligibility: University students and graduates from underrepresented groups including ethnicity, disability, socioeconomic background.

name: Code First Girls
description: Provides free coding courses and nanodegrees to women and non-binary people, with employer partnerships offering direct routes into tech careers.
eligibility: Women and non-binary individuals interested in a career in tech, regardless of prior coding experience.

name: Bright Network Diversity Programmes
description: Connects diverse graduates with leading employers. Runs dedicated insight days and virtual internship programmes for underrepresented groups in finance, law, consulting, and tech.
eligibility: Final-year students and recent graduates from underrepresented backgrounds.

name: Social Mobility Foundation Aspiring Professionals Programme
description: Year-long programme providing Year 12 students from low-income backgrounds with internships, university preparation, and mentoring from professionals at top companies.
eligibility: Year 12 students from households earning under £25,000 per year.

name: Disability Rights UK Career Development Programme
description: Supports disabled students and graduates in accessing careers through mentoring, employer introductions, and advocacy for workplace adjustments.
eligibility: Students and graduates with a physical, mental, or hidden disability.

name: LGBTQ+ Graduates Network Mentoring
description: Connects LGBTQ+ students and recent graduates with out professionals and LGBTQ+-inclusive employers across finance, law, consulting, and technology.
eligibility: LGBTQ+ students and graduates at any stage of their career journey.
